steps.text: Calculate the E value using the formula

questions.question:
Rx:
Pilocarpine nitrate 0.3 g
Sodium chloride q.s.
Purified water ad 15 mL

How many miligrams of sodium chloride should be used in compounding the above Rx? Pilocarpine nitrate E value = 0.23. Round the final answer to the nearest whole number.

steps.text: Calculate the amount of NaCl required to make the entire solution isotonic
Calculate the amount of NaCl represented by the drug (Pilocarpine nitrate)
Subtract Step 2 from Step 1 to find the amount of NaCl needed

questions.question:
Rx:
Zinc sulfate 0.06 g
Boric acid qs
Purified water ad 30 mL
Make isotonic solution
Sig. drop in eyes

How many milligrams of boric acid should be used in compounding the prescription? E value of zinc sulfate is 0.16; E value of boric acid is 0.52. Round your final answer to the nearest tenth.

steps.text: Calculate the amount of NaCl required to make the entire solution isotonic
Calculate the amount of NaCl represented by the drug (Zinc sulfate)
Subtract Step 2 from Step 1 to find the amount of NaCl needed
Convert NaCl amount from step 3 to the amount of the other agent (boric acid)
steps.hint: Amount of boric acid = 0.2604 g NaCl × (1 g boric acid / 0.52 g NaCl) = 0.5008 g = 500.8 mg of boric acid

Calculate the amount of NaCl represented by the drug (Procaine HCl)
Subtract Step 2 from Step 1 to find the amount of NaCl needed

steps.hint: Amount of boric acid = 0.345 g NaCl × (1 g boric acid / 0.52 g NaCl) = 0.66 g of boric acid

questions.question:
Rx:
Potassium Penicillin G 0.45 g
Dextrose, monohydrate q.s
Purified Water ad 25 ml
Make isotonic sol.
Sig. Use as directed

How many milligrams of dextrose monohydrate should be used to compound this prescription? (E value of Potassium penicillin G = 0.18; E value of Dextrose monohydrate = 0.16). Round the final answer to the nearest whole number.

steps.text: Calculate the amount of NaCl required to make the entire solution isotonic
Calculate the amount of NaCl represented by the drug (Potassium Penicillin G)
Subtract Step 2 from Step 1 to find the amount of NaCl needed
Convert NaCl amount from step 3 to the amount of the other agent (dextrose monohydrate)
steps.hint: Amount of dextrose monohydrate = 0.144 g NaCl × (1 g dextrose monohydrate / 0.16 g NaCl) = 0.9 g = 900 mg of dextrose monohydrate
